section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 2: Sectors of the Indian Economy

questions.question: Name the three sectors of the Indian economy.
questions.explanation: The economy is divided based on the nature of activities into primary (extraction), secondary (manufacturing) and tertiary (services).

questions.question: Which sector is also known as the agricultural sector?
questions.explanation: The primary sector involves activities related to natural resources such as agriculture, fishing and mining.

questions.question: Which sector converts raw materials into finished goods?
questions.explanation: The secondary sector includes manufacturing and industrial activities that transform raw materials into products.

questions.question: Which sector provides services like banking and transport?
questions.explanation: The tertiary sector provides services that support production and distribution activities.

questions.question: What does GDP stand for?
questions.explanation: GDP is the total value of all final goods and services produced within a country in a year.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the highest to India’s GDP?
questions.explanation: In India, the service sector contributes the largest share to the GDP.

questions.question: What is disguised unemployment?
questions.explanation: Disguised unemployment occurs when extra workers are engaged in an activity without increasing productivity.

questions.question: Which sector includes government jobs?
questions.explanation: The organised sector includes registered companies that follow government rules and provide job security.

questions.question: What is the main aim of the public sector?
questions.explanation: Public sector enterprises are owned by the government and aim to serve the public interest.

questions.question: Give one example of an unorganised sector job.
questions.explanation: Unorganised sector jobs are not registered and often lack job security and benefits.

notes.title: Primary Sector
notes.content: The primary sector involves activities related to extraction of natural resources such as farming, fishing, forestry and mining.

notes.title: Secondary Sector
notes.content: The secondary sector includes industries and manufacturing units that convert raw materials into finished goods.

notes.title: Tertiary Sector
notes.content: The tertiary sector provides services such as education, healthcare, transport, communication and banking.

notes.title: Organised vs Unorganised Sector
notes.content: The organised sector follows government regulations and provides job security, while the unorganised sector lacks formal protection.

notes.title: Public and Private Sector
notes.content: Public sector is owned by government for welfare, while private sector is owned by individuals for profit.

facts.fact: The tertiary sector has become the largest contributor to India’s GDP in recent years.
A large percentage of India’s population is still dependent on agriculture for employment.
Disguised unemployment is commonly found in rural agricultural areas.
Government hospitals and schools are part of the public sector.
Workers in the unorganised sector often do not receive fixed wages or job security.
